page.title: Tools & Permissions
page.subtitle: Review what AI-Me and AI workers can call, what requires approval, and what is intentionally blocked.

workspace_read.name: Read work items and context
workspace_read.description: AI-Me and workers can read issues, comments, activity, and required workspace context.
workspace_read.audit: Read actions are traceable through activity, approval evidence, and worker run logs.

assign_worker.name: Assign AI workers
assign_worker.description: Hand an issue to Codex, Claude Code, or another worker and enqueue an agent task.
assign_worker.audit: Execution results are recorded after approval.

runtime_execution.name: Call local/cloud runtimes
runtime_execution.description: Workers use registered runtimes for code reading, edits, tests, and task reports.
runtime_execution.audit: Runtime activity is captured in execution logs and worker activity.

post_internal_comment.name: Post internal comments
post_internal_comment.description: Write AI-Me drafted internal notes to issue comments as team-visible records.
post_internal_comment.audit: The approval center keeps the original payload, final payload, and comment ID.

feishu_messages.name: Feishu messages
feishu_messages.description: Receive Feishu messages into AI-Me and send approved bot replies back to the original message.
feishu_messages.audit: Inbound messages enter the exception inbox; outbound replies always require approval.

external_message.name: Send external messages
external_message.description: External communication, commitments, and replies must go through approval first.
external_message.audit: External sends record channel, message ID, and execution failure details.

skills_context.name: Use skill context
skills_context.description: Workers use bound skills for SOPs and operating instructions; skill management remains permission-controlled.
skills_context.audit: Skill changes are tracked by the skills page and worker configuration.

memory_external_use.name: Use memory externally
memory_external_use.description: with_approval memories may support internal drafts, but final external use requires approval.
memory_external_use.audit: Approval evidence should reference every memory ID used.

merge_pull_request.name: Merge pull requests
merge_pull_request.description: v0.1 keeps this as a policy placeholder and does not execute GitHub merges directly.
merge_pull_request.audit: Define permission policy and audit records before enabling this integration.

production_deploy.name: Production deploy
production_deploy.description: Production deployment is irreversible and high risk; v0.1 does not allow automatic execution.
production_deploy.audit: Even after future integration, this action must always require approval.

detail.empty: Select a tool to inspect its permission policy.

error.title: Failed to load tools and permissions
error.description: Some workspace resources could not be loaded.
